none: I-210 proposed construction, Los Angeles : environmental impact statement.

The proposed project involves constructing a 5.6-mile segment of the Interstate Route 210 Freeway in Los Angeles, California, between Van Nuys Boulevard and Sunland Boulevard. This segment is the missing link in a larger 48.7-mile freeway system. The project aims to improve traffic flow, provide a high-speed route for people and goods, and enhance access to regional parks and business centers. However, it will displace families, affect parklands, and potentially increase noise and pollution in the area.
I-210 proposed construction, Los Angeles : environmental impact statement.

The proposed project involves constructing a 5.6-mile segment of the Interstate Route 210 Freeway in Los Angeles, California, between Van Nuys Boulevard and Sunland Boulevard. This segment is the missing link in a larger 48.7-mile freeway system. The project aims to improve traffic flow, provide a high-speed route for people and goods, and enhance access to regional parks and business centers. However, it will displace families, affect parklands, and potentially increase noise and pollution in the area.
Los Angeles, California, between Van Nuys Boulevard and Sunland Boulevard

The proposed construction of the I-210 segment was aimed at completing a critical link in the freeway system to alleviate traffic congestion and improve access to various regions in Los Angeles. However, the project faced significant public pushback due to concerns about displacement of families, environmental impacts on local parklands, and potential increases in noise and pollution. Ultimately, the project was completed, but not without controversy and ongoing discussions about its effects on the community.
"The wall has brought some slight improvement, but we still would like air conditioning for the classrooms." — Pacoima Junior High School community
"FACES fears that busing to other areas might be a possibility." — FACES (Freeway Action for Children's Environment and Safety)
